news.title: Public Health & Society major draws strong student interest
news.summary_en: The Arts & Sciences-based program meets rising demand, combining rigorous interdisciplinary study with hands-on community experience, preparing students to tackle complex health challenges across fields.

news.title: Charlie White appointed dean of Sam Fox School
news.summary_en: Charlie White, the highly regarded artist and academic leader whose vision and student advocacy have helped to build nationally recognized programs at Carnegie Mellon University and the University of Southern California, has been named the Ralph J. Nagel Dean of WashU’s Sam Fox School, announced Chancellor Andrew D. Martin.

news.title: Class Acts: Cela Lopez
news.summary_en: WashU senior Cela Lopez is studying political science so, of course, she’s interested in politics and policy. But what she really cares about is how we think about those topics.

news.title: Class Acts: Beverly Lobo and Jaden Lanza
news.summary_en: WashU Law students Beverly Lobo and Jaden Lanza are conducting a large-scale empirical research project that sits at the intersection of law and data science and could impact imprisonment in America.

news.title: Braver elected to American Academy of Arts & Sciences
news.summary_en: WashU neuroscientist Todd Braver has been elected to the American Academy of Arts & Sciences, one of the nation’s most prestigious honorary societies.

news.title: Huang installed as inaugural Davie professor
news.summary_en: Eric Huang, MD, PhD, a leader in the study of human brain development, has been named the inaugural Joseph M. Davie Distinguished Professor in Pathology & Immunology at WashU Medicine.

news.title: Looser mortgage lending rules, regulation will destabilize financial system
news.summary_en: A new executive order signed by President Donald Trump threatens to destabilize the financial system by reducing lending regulations and rolling back oversight, according to Brittany Lewis at WashU Olin Business School.
